name: VS Code Tunnel On Slurm
summary: Run VS Code Remote Tunnels from short Slurm compute allocations.
description: This skill provides a conservative pattern for starting VS Code Remote Tunnels on allocated Slurm compute nodes, with explicit site-policy checks, plan-only defaults, and cleanup notes.

tools.name: code
tools.notes: VS Code CLI with the tunnel command available on the compute node.

tools.name: sbatch
tools.notes: Used to launch the tunnel inside a scheduled allocation.

tools.name: squeue
tools.notes: Useful for confirming and cancelling the interactive tunnel job.

inputs.name: project_dir
inputs.description: Project directory to open from the allocated compute node.

inputs.name: tunnel_name
inputs.description: Short tunnel name that avoids exposing private project, host, or user details.

inputs.name: time_limit
inputs.description: Short wall time for the tunnel allocation.

inputs.name: site_policy
inputs.description: Local policy confirming whether VS Code tunnels and outbound tunnel services are allowed.

outputs.name: tunnel_log
outputs.description: Slurm output showing the compute node, tunnel name, selected project directory, and VS Code CLI output.

outputs.name: cleanup_notes
outputs.description: Steps for cancelling the Slurm job and unregistering stale tunnel state when appropriate.

examples.title: Plan or start a VS Code tunnel inside a Slurm job
Check VS Code CLI and Slurm prerequisites
Review site policy before enabling tunnels

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
After local policy approval, submit a short debug job with RUN_VSCODE_TUNNEL=1, confirm the log shows a VS Code tunnel URL or connection instructions, then cancel the job.
